aiml_problems.problemStatement: The task involves classifying various outdoor scenes captured in images based on their visual characteristics. Given a dataset of image segments described by 19 numerical visual features, the goal is to accurately classify these segments into one of seven classes representing different outdoor scenes: sky, foliage, cement, window, path, grass, and brickface. This problem is critical for applications such as urban planning, environmental monitoring, and autonomous vehicle navigation systems that require understanding and categorizing outdoor environments.
tasks: Task 1: Data Loading and Inspection — load the dataset. Display sample images from each class (at least 5 per class). Report the total number of images, image dimensions, colour channels, and class distribution. Check for any corrupted or unusually sized images.

Task 2: Image Preprocessing and Augmentation — resize all images to 224x224 pixels and normalise pixel values between 0 and 1. Apply data augmentation techniques including random flips, rotations, and zooms to the training set only. Split the dataset into training and testing sets at an 80/20 ratio with stratified sampling to maintain class balance. Report class balance after splitting.

Task 3: Visual EDA — display a grid of sample images from each class. Plot the class distribution as a bar chart. For tabular metadata (if available), plot distributions of key attributes such as area percentage of certain textures. Visualise mean and variance images per class to understand typical patterns within each category.

Task 4: Model Training — train a baseline CNN using the VGG16 architecture and a pretrained model like MobileNetV2 with transfer learning. Evaluate both models using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. Plot a confusion matrix to visualize misclassifications.

Task 5: Model Comparison and Domain Insights — use feature importance methods such as SHAP values or permutation importance to identify the top 5 most predictive features among the 19 visual features provided. Explain what each of these features implies in terms of real-world outdoor scene characteristics.
preprocessing_requirements: resize images to 224x224 pixels
normalise pixel values between 0 and 1
apply data augmentation (random flips, rotations, and zooms) to the training set
split the dataset into training and testing sets with stratified sampling to maintain class balance

VGG16 is chosen due to its deep architecture which can capture complex visual features effectively. MobileNetV2 benefits from its efficiency and ability to handle large datasets while maintaining good performance through depth-wise separable convolutions.

This will help in filtering out unwanted messages and improving user experience by reducing clutter in their inbox. The dataset consists of 5574 SMS messages, where 4827 are Ham and 747 are Spam, demonstrating a significant class imbalance which makes it a challenging yet interesting problem for text classification in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

If more advanced models are considered, a lightweight transformer like BERT can be fine-tuned to capture more complex patterns in the text but may require more computational resources.
